title: CBC to reopen Jerusalem and Beijing bureaus amid world news expansion - CBC
summary: The Canadian Broadcasting Corporation is significantly expanding its international reporting capabilities by re-establishing bureaus in Jerusalem and Beijing. This strategic move aims to enhance the network's coverage of critical geopolitical developments and provide deeper insights into global affairs.

title: BBC reveals record-breaking global audience figures of over half a billion - BBC
summary: The British Broadcasting Corporation has announced unprecedented global audience numbers, now reaching more than 500 million people worldwide. This milestone underscores the enduring demand for trusted news and diverse programming from international broadcasters.

title: Top 50 English-language news sites in the world: Indian news sites see biggest declines in June - Press Gazette
summary: A recent analysis of the top 50 English-language news websites globally indicates that Indian news outlets experienced the most significant drop in readership during June. This trend suggests a potential shift in international news consumption patterns or increased competition within the digital media landscape.

title: What’s happening this week in economics? - Deloitte
summary: Deloitte has released its weekly economic update, providing insights into the latest global financial trends and market movements. The report highlights key indicators and policy discussions that are shaping the international economic outlook.

title: World News in Brief: Geopolitical tensions impact global economy, Gaza aid funding shortfall, violence in South Sudan, UN condemns deadly mosque attack - UN News
summary: The United Nations has reported on several pressing global issues, including the economic repercussions of ongoing geopolitical tensions and a critical funding deficit for humanitarian aid in Gaza. Additionally, the brief highlighted escalating violence in South Sudan and the UN's strong condemnation of a recent deadly mosque attack.

title: India proposes new digital rules to regulate news and political posts on social media
summary: The Indian government has introduced new digital regulations aimed at overseeing content shared on social media platforms. These rules are designed to control the spread of news and political commentary, potentially impacting online discourse and freedom of expression.

title: MeitY secretary says social media users posting on current affairs can come under I&B oversight
summary: A senior official from the Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) has indicated that individuals sharing current affairs content on social media platforms could fall under the purview of the Information and Broadcasting (I&B) Ministry. This statement suggests a broader interpretation of regulatory authority over online content creators, extending beyond traditional media outlets.

title: Government Notifies Promotion and Regulation of Online Gaming Rules 2026
summary: The Indian government has officially announced new regulations for the online gaming sector, aiming to promote responsible gaming while establishing a clear regulatory framework. These rules are expected to bring greater clarity and oversight to the rapidly expanding digital gaming industry in the country.

title: PM Modi Becomes India’s Longest-Serving Head of Government
summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi has achieved a significant milestone, becoming the longest-serving head of government in India's history. This accomplishment reflects his enduring political tenure and leadership over multiple terms.

title: Understanding the Tenth Schedule and its role in regulating political defections
summary: The Tenth Schedule of the Indian Constitution, commonly known as the Anti-Defection Law, plays a crucial role in preventing political defections by elected representatives. This legal framework aims to ensure stability in legislative bodies and uphold party discipline.

title: IEA Boss Warns Global Economy in Peril If Hormuz Crisis Persists - Bloomberg.com
summary: The head of the International Energy Agency has issued a stark warning about the potential for severe global economic disruption if the crisis in the Strait of Hormuz continues. Prolonged instability in this vital shipping lane could significantly impact energy supplies and international trade, threatening worldwide economic growth.

title: The New Oil? Why the World Is Chasing Copper - The Economic Times
summary: Global demand for copper is surging, leading many to dub it the "new oil" due to its critical role in the green energy transition and advanced technologies. Nations and industries are increasingly prioritizing securing copper supplies, recognizing its indispensable nature for future economic development.

title: Dollar Gains Amid Global Economic Tensions and Market Volatility - Devdiscourse
summary: The U.S. dollar has strengthened significantly, benefiting from heightened global economic uncertainties and widespread market volatility. Investors are increasingly seeking the perceived safety of the dollar as international tensions escalate and financial markets experience turbulence.

title: Stock market today: Dow, S&P 500, Nasdaq post weekly losses as semiconductors get smoked - Yahoo Finance UK
summary: Major U.S. stock indices, including the Dow, S&P 500, and Nasdaq, concluded the week with significant losses, primarily driven by a sharp downturn in semiconductor stocks. This broad market retreat reflects growing investor concerns, particularly within the technology sector.

title: Debt, AI boom and economic fragilities raise global risks, BIS says - CNBC
summary: The Bank for International Settlements (BIS) has highlighted increasing global economic vulnerabilities stemming from rising debt levels and the rapid expansion of the artificial intelligence sector. These factors, combined with existing economic fragilities, pose significant new risks to worldwide financial stability.

title: Nasdaq closes down more than 2% as global tech sell-off deepens - The Washington Post
summary: The Nasdaq Composite index experienced a significant decline, falling over 2% as a broader global sell-off in technology stocks intensified. This downturn reflects widespread investor apprehension regarding the tech sector's valuations and future growth prospects.

title: MARKET_TICKER
Stock Market Holiday Alert: NSE, BSE To Remain Closed For Eight Days In July 2026; Check Full List - NDTV Profit
summary: The Indian stock exchanges, NSE and BSE, are scheduled for multiple closures throughout July 2026, impacting trading days. Investors should consult the official holiday calendar to plan their activities accordingly.

title: Buy or sell: Sumeet Bagadia recommends three stocks to buy on Monday - 20 July 2026 - livemint.com
summary: A financial expert has provided specific stock recommendations for investors to consider for Monday's trading session. These suggestions include potential buy or sell opportunities across three different companies.

title: Stocks to buy under ₹200: Mehul Kothari of Anand Rathi recommends three shares to buy or sell - livemint.com
summary: An analyst from Anand Rathi has identified three shares priced under ₹200, offering recommendations for both buying and selling. This advice aims to guide investors looking for opportunities in the affordable segment of the market.

title: From Gift Nifty, US chip stocks selloff to oil prices: 10 key things that changed for Indian stock market overnight - livemint.com
summary: Several global factors, including movements in Gift Nifty, a selloff in US chip stocks, and fluctuating oil prices, are poised to influence the Indian stock market. These overnight developments provide crucial context for today's trading session.

title: AI Agents in Financial Services Market Size to Hit USD 6.54 Billion by 2035 - Precedence Research
summary: The market for AI agents within financial services is projected for substantial growth, expected to reach $6.54 billion by 2035. This forecast highlights the increasing integration and reliance on intelligent automation across various financial operations.

title: How banks are using AI in financial crime and compliance - FinTech Global
summary: Financial institutions are increasingly deploying artificial intelligence to combat sophisticated financial crimes and enhance regulatory compliance. AI-powered systems offer advanced capabilities for detecting fraud, money laundering, and other illicit activities, thereby strengthening security measures.

title: Nigeria Central Bank Mandates AI Tools for AML Monitoring - Dabafinance
summary: The Central Bank of Nigeria has issued a directive requiring financial institutions to adopt AI tools for anti-money laundering (AML) monitoring. This mandate underscores a growing global trend among regulators to leverage advanced technology in the fight against financial illicit activities.

title: AI in Fintech. 5 Ways Artificial Intelligence Is Changing Banking [Updated] - Netguru
summary: Artificial intelligence is fundamentally reshaping the banking sector by introducing innovative solutions across various operations. From personalized customer experiences to enhanced risk management, AI is driving significant transformation in how financial services are delivered.

title: Fintech & AI: The Rise of Embedded Finance in Everyday Platforms - intlbm
summary: The convergence of fintech and artificial intelligence is fueling the rapid expansion of embedded finance, integrating financial services directly into non-financial platforms. This trend allows for seamless transactions and personalized financial offerings within daily digital interactions.

title: 34 Examples of AI in Finance 2026 - Built In
summary: A comprehensive overview highlights numerous applications of artificial intelligence across the financial industry in 2026. These examples demonstrate AI's diverse capabilities, from automating routine tasks to providing sophisticated analytical insights for decision-making.

title: India’s AI Revolution Accelerates as Billion-Dollar Investments Reshape the Artificial Intelligence Market - Spherical Insights
summary: India is experiencing a rapid acceleration in its artificial intelligence sector, fueled by substantial investments totaling billions of dollars. This influx of capital is fundamentally transforming the nation's AI market landscape, positioning India as a significant player in the global AI economy.

title: India’s hosts AI Impact Summit, drawing world leaders, tech giants - Al Jazeera
summary: India recently hosted a major AI Impact Summit, attracting prominent world leaders and leading technology companies. This event underscored India's growing ambition to establish itself as a central hub for artificial intelligence innovation and collaboration on the global stage.

title: Artificial Intelligence and Future of India's IT Companies - Economic and Political Weekly
summary: Artificial intelligence is poised to significantly reshape the landscape and operational models of India's prominent IT companies. This technological shift presents both immense opportunities for innovation and considerable challenges for the future of white-collar work within the sector.

title: India Issues 2025 AI Governance Guidelines- How It Compares to Other Global AI Acts - The National Law Review
summary: India has released its 2025 AI Governance Guidelines, establishing a framework for the responsible development and deployment of artificial intelligence within the country. These guidelines are being critically examined to understand how they align with or diverge from existing global AI regulatory acts.

title: Towards responsible AI for mental health and well-being: experts chart a way forward - World Health Organization (WHO)
summary: Experts convened by the World Health Organization are actively developing strategies to ensure the responsible implementation of artificial intelligence in mental health and well-being applications. This initiative aims to harness AI's potential benefits while mitigating associated risks and ethical concerns in healthcare.

title: Global AI Adoption in 2025 – AI Economy Institute - Microsoft
summary: A report from the AI Economy Institute, supported by Microsoft, provides insights into the projected trends and patterns of global AI adoption throughout 2025. This research highlights the increasing integration of artificial intelligence across various industries worldwide, signaling its growing economic significance.
